prerequisites: An active Reply.io account with access to the workspace whose data you want to extract.
Permission to view account settings in Reply.io, which is where the API key is generated.
A Reply.io API key, created from Settings then API Key in the Reply.io app. This value is supplied as the connector's api_key field.

authMethods.name: API key
authMethods.summary: Reply.io authenticates every request with a single API key, passed in the Authorization header as a Bearer token (Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY). Generate the key under Settings then API Key in the Reply.io app and provide it as the connector's required api_key field. Requests are rate limited to 100 per minute and 3,000 per hour per user, with stricter limits on reporting and statistics endpoints; exceeding them returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Reply.io API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Reply.io and open Settings then API Key. Create a new key (or copy an existing one). This key authorizes API access to your Reply.io workspace and is the only credential the connector needs.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated key into the connector's api_key field. This is the single required configuration value. The connector sends it on each request as an Authorization Bearer token to authenticate against the Reply.io API.

setupSteps.title: Verify access
setupSteps.body: Confirm the key works by running a read against your Reply.io account. If you receive HTTP 401 the key is invalid or missing; if you receive HTTP 429 you have hit the rate limit (100 requests/minute or 3,000 requests/hour per user), so honor the Retry-After header before retrying.
